commit.message:
Add personal best tracking with confetti animation

Backend:
- /api/submit now returns is_new_pb (bool) and prev_best, computed by
  combining COUNT+MAX into a single pre-insert query (no schema change)

Frontend:
- PB badge on each unlocked scene card (⭐ PB: 87%) using best_scores
  from userProgress, updates on every renderCards() call
- #pbBanner inside score panel — shows "⭐ New Personal Best!" when
  is_new_pb is true, gold animated pill with scale-in keyframe
- showPBBlast() — 70-piece CSS confetti burst + large gold text overlay,
  auto-removes after 3.2s; fires alongside the score panel banner
- Personal Bests section in My Progress modal: scenes sorted by best
  score descending, shows ↑ arrow when latest attempt beats previous one

commit.message:
Add friend challenge system

Backend:
- challenges table (challenge_id, challenger_username, scene_id, score_to_beat)
- POST /api/challenge — creates a challenge link, returns short hex ID + URL
- GET /api/challenge/{id} — returns challenge details (public, no auth)
- GET /challenge/{id} — SPA route: serves index.html so JS handles routing

Frontend:
- URL routing in boot: detects /challenge/[id] path, loads challenge screen
- #challengeScreen — full-page challenge card showing challenger, score, movie
- "Accept Challenge" button opens scene modal with challengeCtx set
- After scoring: showChallengeResult() shows YOU WON / So close overlay
- "⚔ Challenge a Friend" button in score panel → POST to create challenge
- Share panel: copy link + WhatsApp share button
- Login/register from challenge page flows back into challenge after auth

commit.message:
Fix order-service startup crash: remove invalid Session dependency

db: Session = Depends(get_db) if False else None evaluated to
db: Session = None, making FastAPI treat Session as a Pydantic
query parameter. Remove the unused db param since the function
creates its own SessionLocal() directly.

repository.name: tarun-store
repository.description: Tarun's Store — Luxury e-commerce platform with Datadog SSI instrumentation

commit.message:
fix: sync playback engine with edits and fix recording position/live preview

- PlaybackController.updateProject: debounced 50ms reschedule that always
  fires (was stopped-only), so edits, recordings, and AI generation are
  immediately playable without requiring a stop/play cycle
- editStore.syncToProjectStore: call updateProject after every note edit
  so piano roll changes are audible on next play without full reload
- midiInputStore.startRecordingWithCountIn: capture Transport ticks before
  count-in so recording starts from current position (paused or seeked),
  not hardcoded tick 0
- TransportStrip: trigger count-in+record from paused state (was stopped-only)
- midiInputStore: live-flush completedNotes to projectStore on each note-off
  so notes appear in the piano roll in real-time during recording
- midiInputStore: snapshot preRecordingNotes at startRecording to prevent
  double-counting when commitRecording merges with the live-flushed state
- midiInputStore: cancel live preview (restore preRecordingNotes) when
  recording is cancelled via disarm or stopRecording without commit

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix promo code flow: redeem code before generating plan

Promo code was redeemed AFTER generate-plan, but generate-plan
requires an active subscription — so promo users hit a paywall.
Now the code is redeemed first, activating the subscription,
then the plan generates successfully.

commit.message:
feat: add /kg-pack skill — knowledge pack manager for any Claude Code project

Adds a user-invocable /kg-pack skill that works as a package manager for
domain-expert knowledge:

  /kg-pack list                    — show available packs (49 domains)
  /kg-pack install rust-expert     — install as Claude Code skill
  /kg-pack build "my topic"        — build new pack from topic
  /kg-pack build-from-urls file.md — build from URL list
  /kg-pack query pack "question"   — query a pack's KG directly
  /kg-pack update name             — rebuild with latest docs
  /kg-pack info name               — show pack details
  /kg-pack uninstall name          — remove skill

Two versions:
- .claude/skills/kg-pack/SKILL.md — project-local (gitignored)
- skills/kg-pack/SKILL.md — distributable, copy to any project

commit.message:
Add image tile mode, default to 6x6 grid

Add a scrollable preset gallery of 8 public-domain artworks sourced
from Wikimedia Commons (Van Gogh, Hokusai, Monet, Klimt, Vermeer,
NASA). Thumbnails are loaded lazily at 100px; the full image is
fetched with crossOrigin=anonymous when selected so it can be drawn
to a canvas. The active preset is highlighted and deselected on
Clear Image or when the user uploads their own photo.

Shared applyLoadedImage() helper consolidates the logic that was
previously duplicated between the upload and preset paths.

commit.message:
Fix overscan crashes, NearestFilter everywhere, scaling audit across 150+ elements

Overscan adjustment crashed because dispose() was called on elements still
queued for deferred build. Added _built flag to skip dispose for unbuilt
elements. Fixed Shift+Arrow X/Y nudge (case labels didn't match lowercased
key). Added minimum dimension guards throughout the pipeline. Switched all
87 canvas textures from LinearFilter to NearestFilter. Fixed canvas aspect
ratio stretching, proportional point sizes, bounds fitting, and visual
density across ~150 elements.

fix: only downsample segmentation overlay when fast mode is enabled

rendering. Now full-resolution output is used by default, with
downsampling only applied when fast mode is active.

commit.message:
Add IndexedDB per-ticker chart cache for fast reload

Caches parsed chart data (closes, longName, currency) per ticker
in IndexedDB with daily expiry. On tab load, cached tickers are
resolved instantly from IDB without network requests. This means:
- Page refresh mid-fetch resumes where it left off
- Clearing tab-level cache still has per-ticker data available
- Second load of same day is near-instant even without tab cache
Old entries are cleaned up automatically on page load.

commit.message:
Add 21 self-improvement training pairs (p400-p407): autonomous AI self-training pipeline

Core "get smarter" system for local AI on 15GB VRAM:
- p400: Self-training loop, quality filtering, adaptive curriculum, deep thinking
- p401: QLoRA pipeline (NF4 + 8-bit AdamW), LoRA merge cycling, novel code generation
- p402: Execution-based reward model, self-play arena, active learning
- p403: GGUF conversion/quantization, llama-server optimization, LoRA merge-to-GGUF
- p404: LoRA bank, few-shot adaptation, curriculum meta-learning
- p405: Error pattern analysis, failure replay buffer, automated test generation
- p406: Online distillation loop, progressive knowledge building with dependency graph
- p407: Complete "get smarter" orchestrator, improvement monitoring/analytics

commit 5b26b92d2646f9b423aec6b03ee983e43d21a03c
Author: Thadeu Melo <thadeu_afmelo@hotmail.com>

    - Add center1/is_moving to Sphere and SceneSphere for moving spheres

commit 506fadfa5a8a5917fa8142ffbb528a67c89e54c2
Author: Thadeu Melo <thadeu_afmelo@hotmail.com>
Date:   Thu Mar 5 00:38:15 2026 -0300

    Implement save changes modal for unsaved scene edits (#59)

    * Enhance file import/export functionality with fallback support

    - Updated `cmd_action_file_import` and `cmd_action_file_save_as` to utilize native file dialogs with a fallback to a modal dialog if the native option fails.
    - Introduced `file_import_from_path` and `file_save_as_path` procedures to handle scene loading and saving, improving code organization and reusability.
    - Refactored `file_modal_confirm` to streamline the import and save operations, delegating the logic to the new procedures for better clarity and maintainability.
    - Updated documentation in `AGENTS.md` to include new native dialog functionalities.

    These changes enhance the user experience by providing more robust file handling capabilities in the editor.

    * Implement save changes modal for unsaved scene edits

    - Introduced a new `SaveChangesModalState` structure to manage the save changes modal, allowing users to choose between saving, saving as, canceling, or continuing when there are unsaved changes.
    - Updated various command actions to trigger the save changes modal when necessary, including file import, exit, and scene loading.
    - Added `mark_scene_dirty` function to track unsaved changes across different editing actions, ensuring the modal is displayed appropriately.
    - Enhanced the user experience by preventing accidental loss of changes when navigating between scenes or exiting the application.

    * Refactor file save functionality to improve error handling and user feedback

    - Updated `cmd_action_file_save` to return a boolean indicating success or failure, enhancing error handling during save operations.
    - Introduced a wrapper function `cmd_action_file_save_menu` for menu binding, maintaining the original command structure while improving clarity.
    - Modified `file_save_as_path` to return a boolean for success or failure, ensuring consistent error handling across file operations.
    - Enhanced the save changes modal to keep open on save failure, improving user experience by preventing accidental data loss.
    - Adjusted confirm modal logic to enable save/load actions only when the scene is dirty, ensuring users are prompted appropriately before replacing unsaved changes.

    * Enhance camera editing functionality and improve file modal handling

    - Added a single definition for `FILE_MODAL_FALLBACK` to streamline fallback logic for text path modal when native dialogs are unavailable.
    - Introduced `cam_drag_before_params` to `EditViewState` for better tracking of camera parameters during drag operations, enabling accurate undo functionality.
    - Updated camera drag and rotation handling in `update_edit_view_content` to push edit history and mark the scene as dirty, ensuring changes are tracked effectively.
    - Improved the `save_file_dialog` function to combine `default_dir` and `default_name` for a more intuitive initial filename suggestion in the file dialog.

    These changes enhance user experience by providing more robust camera controls and improved file handling capabilities.

    * Remove unused `load_example_scene_direct` function from the confirm modal, streamlining the codebase and improving maintainability. This change eliminates unnecessary complexity in scene loading logic.

    * Remove deprecated JSON scene files `test.json` and `test1.json` to clean up the project and eliminate unused resources.

    * Enhance file handling and documentation for scene management

    - Updated `.gitignore` to include user scene files while preserving the `scenes/` directory with `.gitkeep`.
    - Improved `CLAUDE.md` to clarify the default directory for file dialogs and added details about the `FILE_MODAL_FALLBACK` feature.
    - Enhanced `AGENTS.md` to reflect changes in file dialog handling and the introduction of the `Save Changes` modal for unsaved edits.
    - Updated `native_dialog.odin` to ensure the default directory for file dialogs is set to `scenes` when no current file is specified.

    These changes improve the user experience by providing clearer documentation and more robust file handling capabilities in the editor.

    * Update save changes modal logic to prevent silent discards

    - Modified the confirm modal behavior to ensure that pressing Enter when no file path exists triggers a "Save As" action instead of a "Continue" action. This change aims to enhance user experience by preventing accidental loss of unsaved changes and promoting explicit saving actions.

    * Fix memory leaks, enforce .json extension, clarify shared drag field

    - dialog_default_dir: free get_current_directory() result before returning
      filepath.clean copy (was leaking the heap allocation)
    - file_modal_confirm: delete cloned text on empty input for Import and
      SaveAs cases, consistent with PresetName
    - file_save_as_path: auto-append .json when caller omits the extension
      (Linux zenity and macOS choose-file-name don't enforce it)
    - cam_drag_before_params: replace terse comment with block comment
      clarifying it is shared across mutually exclusive drag types

    Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

    ---------

    Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit 841f9b47cf67663dfb676a6b4db774d0afb6c6e1
Author: Thadeu Melo <thadeu_afmelo@hotmail.com>
Date:   Wed Mar 4 13:55:04 2026 -0300

    Add Examples menu with Weekend Final Scene (#56) (#57)

    Adds a hardcoded Examples menu to the menu bar with the "Weekend Final
    Scene" from Ray Tracing in One Weekend. Loading uses a fixed RNG seed
    (42) for deterministic output and prompts the user to confirm before
    replacing the current scene.

    Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refactor ray and sphere structures for improved clarity and consistency

- Updated the `ray` struct to use named fields for `origin`, `dir`, and `time` for better readability.
- Changed `Sphere` and `SceneSphere` to use `[3]f32` for `center` and `center1`, removing the previous `Ray` type.
- Adjusted related functions and procedures to accommodate the new structure definitions, ensuring consistent handling of sphere positions and ray properties.
- Enhanced the `hit_sphere` function to utilize the new `sphere_center_at` for accurate position calculations during ray intersection tests.

* Fix motion blur implementation: revert broken ray types, add GPU sphere_center_at

- Remove duplicate `time` field from `ray` struct (vector3.odin)
- Revert Sphere/Cube center fields from `ray` back to `[3]f32` (hittable.odin)
- Revert SceneSphere center fields from `core.Ray` back to `[3]f32` (core/types.odin)
- Remove unused `Ray` struct from core/types.odin and `ray_time` field from SceneSphere
- Fix hit_sphere to use sphere_center_at(sphere, r.time) instead of ray_at(r, r.time)
- Fix `orig` → `origin` field name in material.odin and camera.odin ray literals
- Fix scene_build.odin to assign s.center/s.center1 directly instead of constructing ray{}
- Fix gpu_types.odin: build GPURay from center/center1 and set _pad[0] as is_moving flag
- Add sphere_center_at helper to raytrace.comp and fix hit_sphere for GPU motion blur

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix GPU sphere layout mismatch: add std430 padding to GPURay and GPUSphere

GLSL vec3 under std430 has base alignment 16, so a 4-byte implicit pad
sits between the origin and dir fields of the Ray struct. Without this,
the CPU (#packed, 28 bytes) and GPU (std430, 32 bytes) diverged, causing:
  - center.dir/time read from wrong offsets (4-byte shift)
  - albedo read from wrong offset (16-byte shift) → always black
  - mat_type/fuzz_or_ior/_pad read from wrong offsets
  - _pad[0] (is_moving flag) written at CPU offset 52, read by GPU
    at offset 68 → random non-zero values → static spheres treated as
    moving with garbage velocities

Fix:
  - GPURay: add _pad0: f32 after origin → 32 bytes (was 28, #packed)
  - GPUSphere: add _pad_r: [3]f32 after radius → 80 bytes (was 64)

Both sizes now verified to match the GLSL std430 Sphere/Ray structs.
sphere_center_at and the is_moving flag in _pad[0] now work correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Implement motion blur support for spheres and camera shutter parameters

- Added motion blur functionality to `Sphere` and `SceneSphere`, allowing spheres to interpolate between `center` and `center1` over a defined shutter interval.
- Introduced `shutter_open` and `shutter_close` parameters in `CameraParams` for motion blur timing, with UI adjustments in the camera and object properties panels to expose these settings.
- Updated scene persistence to save and load new motion blur fields, ensuring backward compatibility with older scene files.
- Enhanced the editor UI to include motion offset controls for spheres and updated the camera panel to reflect shutter settings.

This commit enhances the rendering capabilities by allowing for realistic motion blur effects in the scene.

* Enhance motion blur functionality and camera shutter integration

- Updated `get_ray` to sample ray time within the `shutter_open` and `shutter_close` interval, enabling motion blur effects based on camera settings.
- Improved UI for sphere motion parameters, allowing users to adjust motion offsets directly in the Object Properties panel.
- Refactored variable names for clarity, ensuring consistent handling of motion offsets in the editor.
- Enhanced scene persistence to save and load new camera shutter parameters and sphere motion states, maintaining compatibility with older scene files.

These changes significantly improve the rendering realism by accurately simulating motion blur in the scene.

* Comment

---------

commit.message:
fix: comprehensive security hardening — RBAC, JWT, rate limiting, input validation (#125)

- Startup validator: fail on default secrets in non-dev profiles
- RBAC: @PreAuthorize on 12 controllers (Admin/Operator for writes, authenticated for reads)
- JWT: use DB roles instead of token claims, add token revocation via tokenInvalidatedAt
- JWT: reduce expiry from 24h to 2h, strip PII from claims
- Rate limiting: login (5 attempts/15min) and API (120 req/min per IP)
- Password complexity: require uppercase, lowercase, digit, special char
- File uploads: Apache Tika content-based MIME validation
- SCIM: config-based base URL instead of trusting host headers
- Swagger: conditional permitAll based on config
- CORS: validate origin format, reject wildcards
- CSP + X-XSS-Protection headers
- SAML: HTTPS enforcement warning, tighten auto-linking to SCIM-provisioned users only
- SSO: pass token via URL fragment instead of query param
- Slack webhooks: SSRF prevention via domain validation
- CSV import: 10k row limit
- Dashboard: filter recent activity to exclude login/user events
- Reduce log verbosity for invalid JWT tokens

disable edge middleware — fix SSR routes 404ing on all domains (#90)

chain was breaking route matching, causing x-astro-noop 404s.
